title: Lady Trieu's Uprising
summary: In 248 AD, Trieu Thi Trinh, a daughter of the Cuu Chan land, raised the flag of rebellion against the Wu army, leaving behind immortal words of patriotism.

slides.text: Lady Trieu's real name was Trieu Thi Trinh, a strong woman with a great sense of national pride.
slides.imagePrompt: Young Lady Trieu practicing martial arts in the mountains, determined look, beautiful landscape, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: She is famous for the saying: 'I want to ride the strong wind, tread the fierce waves, and slay the giant whales of the Eastern Sea...'.
slides.imagePrompt: Lady Trieu standing on a high cliff, wind blowing her hair, symbolic image of riding wind and waves, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: She often wore golden armor, walked on ivory clogs, and rode a white elephant as she charged into battle against the Wu army.
slides.imagePrompt: Lady Trieu in golden armor, riding a white elephant with 3 tusks, leading her army to battle, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: Her majestic presence made the enemy tremble whenever they heard her name, calling her the 'Splendid Flower General' (Nhuy Kieu Tuong quan).
slides.imagePrompt: Enemies fleeing in fear as Lady Trieu approaches, heroic and majestic presence, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.question: What color armor did Lady Trieu usually wear into battle?
What did the enemy call Lady Trieu?
